firstReading.text: Rise up in splendor, Jerusalem! Your light has come, the glory of the Lord shines upon you.

psalm.response: Lord, every nation on earth will adore you.

secondReading.text: Brothers and sisters: You have heard of the stewardship of God's grace that was given to me for your benefit.

gospel.text: When Jesus was born in Bethlehem of Judea, in the days of King Herod, behold, magi from the east arrived in Jerusalem.

firstReading.text: Beloved: We receive from him whatever we ask, because we keep his commandments and do what pleases him.

psalm.response: I will give you all the nations for an inheritance.

gospel.text: When Jesus heard that John had been arrested, he withdrew to Galilee.

firstReading.text: Here is my servant whom I uphold, my chosen one with whom I am pleased.

psalm.response: The Lord will bless his people with peace.

secondReading.text: Peter proceeded to speak to those gathered in the house of Cornelius.

gospel.text: The people were filled with expectation, and all were asking in their hearts whether John might be the Christ.
